questions.question: What is the maximum weight limit for India Post Parcel?
questions.explanation: While standard unregistered parcels have a 4 kg limit and registered parcels have a 20 kg limit, Business Parcels and certain international parcels handled by India Post can have a maximum weight limit of up to 35 kg.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the minimum chargeable weight for booking packet under 'Gyan Post'?
questions.explanation: Under the Gyan Post service (a specialized postal product offered in certain circles), the minimum chargeable weight for a packet is 300 grams.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: In POSB account, if no withdrawal/deposit takes place in the account during continuous ___ years, the account shall be treated as silent.
questions.explanation: A Post Office Savings Bank (POSB) account becomes silent or dormant if there are no transactions (deposit or withdrawal) for three consecutive financial years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the minimum amount to open Sukanya Samriddhi Account?
questions.explanation: The minimum deposit required to open a Sukanya Samriddhi Account (SSA) is ₹250, and thereafter deposits can be made in multiples of ₹50.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: In Anticipated Endowment Assurance policy, available policy terms are:
questions.explanation: Under Postal Life Insurance (PLI), the Anticipated Endowment Assurance (Sumangal) policy is available for terms of 15 years and 20 years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Mention the Postal Circle in which Andaman and Nicobar Islands Territory is situated.
questions.explanation: The Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands falls under the administrative jurisdiction of the West Bengal Postal Circle.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: The working of all Post Offices under Postal Divisions is directly controlled by:

options.D: Superintendent or Senior Superintendent of Post Offices

questions.explanation: A Postal Division is headed by a Superintendent of Post Offices (SPOs) or Senior Superintendent of Post Offices (SSPOs), who directly controls the working of all post offices within that division.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: A Post Office which is also opened on Sundays and holidays is called:
questions.explanation: Night Post Offices work for extended hours on working days and also remain open on Sundays and National Holidays, operating with restricted working hours and services.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: As per clause 2 of Post Office Guide Part-I, what are the three classes of Post Offices?
questions.explanation: According to the Post Office Guide Part-I, post offices are divided into three classes: Head Post Offices, Sub-Post Offices, and Branch Post Offices.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: How many holidays are observed in Post Offices in all Circles?
questions.explanation: The Department of Posts observes 17 holidays in a calendar year across all postal circles (14 compulsory and 3 chosen from a restricted list).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the maximum amount limit for issuance of single Money Order?
questions.explanation: The maximum remittance limit for a single inland Electronic Money Order (eMO) is ₹5000.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Under which clause of Post Office Guide Part-I, 'Packing' is mentioned?
questions.explanation: In the Post Office Guide Part-I, Clause 15 specifically deals with the rules and guidelines for 'Packing' of postal articles.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which clause of Post Office Guide Part-I expresses the particulars to be furnished for 'Method of addressing'?
questions.explanation: Clause 26 of the Post Office Guide Part-I provides detailed instructions on the 'Method of addressing' postal articles for proper sorting and delivery.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Postage stamp should be affixed:

options.B: On the back side of the postal article
options.C: On the right-hand top corner of the address side
options.D: On the bottom left-hand side corner

questions.explanation: As per postal regulations, postage stamps should be affixed to the top right-hand corner of the address side of the envelope or postcard.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What kind of articles can we get through Post Boxes?
questions.explanation: Post Boxes are used exclusively for the delivery of fully prepaid unregistered/unaccountable postal articles. Accountable articles like registered letters cannot be delivered through a Post Box.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: If the renter of a Post Box changes his business address to another place, he should invariably inform the Postmaster:
questions.explanation: If a Post Box renter changes their business or residential address, they must inform the Postmaster within 7 days of the change to maintain the facility.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the rent amount for both Post Box and Post Bag for a quarter or part thereof?
questions.explanation: The fee for either a Post Box or a Post Bag alone is ₹50 per quarter. However, the combined fee for renting both a Post Box and a Post Bag is ₹80 per quarter.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: For availing Post Bag facility, the Post Bag along with lock and duplicate key is supplied by:
questions.explanation: For the Post Bag facility, the renter (customer) must supply the bag, lock, and duplicate key at their own cost.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Under which section of Post Office Guide Part-I, Official Postal articles are mentioned?
questions.explanation: Section III of the Post Office Guide Part-I deals with the rules and guidelines regarding Official Postal Articles.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Official postal articles must bear the superscription of:
questions.explanation: All official postal articles must bear the superscription 'On India Government Service' (OIGS) or 'On Service' to distinguish them from private mail.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The postage chargeable on the delivery of official postal articles, when the postage is insufficiently paid, is:
questions.explanation: For unpaid or insufficiently paid official postal articles, the postage chargeable on delivery is equal to the exact amount of deficiency, unlike private mail which is charged double.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following is not prohibited for transmission by Inland Post?
questions.explanation: Sharp instruments are allowed through Inland Post only if they are properly protected and securely packed so they cannot cause injury to postal workers. The other items are strictly prohibited.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the maximum weight for sending a letter?
questions.explanation: The maximum permissible weight for a standard letter sent through India Post is 2 kilograms.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The expression 'Railway Mail Service' means the service responsible for the carriage of mail by:

options.D: By rail, road, air and river

questions.explanation: Although named 'Railway Mail Service', its definition includes the responsibility for the carriage of mails by rail, road, air, and river routes.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which office helps in reduction of number of loose bags?
questions.explanation: A Transit Office encloses several bags directed to the same office into a single Transit Bag, thereby reducing the number of loose bags handled during transit.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which among the following is correct, in respect of Transit Mail Office?

options.A: Closed bags are received and opened
options.C: Sorting of letters is not done

questions.explanation: A Transit Mail Office (TMO) only deals with closed bags. It receives and dispatches closed bags without opening them; hence, no sorting of individual letters takes place.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: AMPC means:
questions.explanation: AMPC stands for Automatic Mail Processing Centre, where large volumes of mail are sorted rapidly using automated machinery.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Circle Bag Office is situated in which office?
questions.explanation: The Circle Bag Office is generally located at the Postal Stores Depot (PSD) at the circle headquarters.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: When Account bag is sent by Head Office to the Sub Post Office, it contains:
questions.explanation: When a Head Post Office sends an Account Bag to a Sub Post Office, it typically contains the SO Slip, whereas the SO Daily Account is sent from the SO to the HO.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: As per Rule 53 of Postal Manual Volume V, how many kinds of Mail Bags are prescribed?
questions.explanation: Rule 53 of Postal Manual Volume V categorizes mail bags into two types: Due Mail (dispatched regularly) and Unusual Mail (dispatched only when required).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What value should come in the place of question mark (?) in the below given question: 8.5 × (14 × 1.5 × 2.5 × 20) ÷ 7 = ?
questions.explanation: First, solve the brackets: 14 × 1.5 = 21, and 2.5 × 20 = 50. Then, 21 × 50 = 1050. The equation becomes: 8.5 × 1050 ÷ 7. Divide 1050 by 7 to get 150. Finally, 8.5 × 150 = 1275.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: What value should come in the place of question mark (?) in the below given question: 5 × 2 + (11 + 8) - 18 ÷ 9 = ?
questions.explanation: Using BODMAS: First solve the brackets (11 + 8) = 19. Next perform division: 18 ÷ 9 = 2. Then multiplication: 5 × 2 = 10. Finally, addition and subtraction: 10 + 19 - 2 = 29 - 2 = 27.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A student saves an average of ₹4000 per month for the first three months. How much should be saved (in ₹) on an average per month over the next 9 months to average ₹4375 per month over the whole year?
questions.explanation: Total savings for the first 3 months = 3 × 4000 = ₹12,000. Total target savings for the year (12 months) = 12 × 4375 = ₹52,500. Required savings for the next 9 months = 52,500 - 12,000 = ₹40,500. Average savings for these 9 months = 40,500 ÷ 9 = ₹4500.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: The speed of a boat in still water is 12 km/h and the stream flows at a speed of 3 km/h. The distance travelled in the boat downstream in 24 minutes is:
questions.explanation: Downstream speed = Speed of boat + Speed of stream = 12 + 3 = 15 km/h. Time = 24 minutes = 24/60 hours = 0.4 hours. Distance = Speed × Time = 15 × 0.4 = 6 km.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: The average of 5 numbers is 38. If one number is excluded, their average is 36. Find the excluded number.
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 numbers = 5 × 38 = 190. Sum of remaining 4 numbers = 4 × 36 = 144. Excluded number = 190 - 144 = 46.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Find the simple interest (in ₹) on ₹2500 as a sum borrowed at 6% per year rate of interest for 4 years.
questions.explanation: Simple Interest (SI) = (Principal × Rate × Time) ÷ 100. SI = (2500 × 6 × 4) ÷ 100 = 60000 ÷ 100 = ₹600.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: In an election between two candidates, one person got 35% of the votes and lost by 36 votes. Assuming that there were no invalid votes, what was the total number of votes?
questions.explanation: Losing candidate got 35%. Winning candidate got 100% - 35% = 65%. Difference in vote percentage = 65% - 35% = 30%. This 30% corresponds to 36 votes. Total votes (100%) = (36 ÷ 30) × 100 = 120.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Arun runs on a circular track having length 400 metres. He makes 3 rounds daily on it. How much distance (in km) does he cover on the track in a week?
questions.explanation: Distance covered in one day = 3 × 400 m = 1200 m = 1.2 km. Distance covered in a week (7 days) = 1.2 km × 7 = 8.4 km.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Ramesh went to a brick kiln to buy bricks. The rate of bricks was ₹2500 per one thousand bricks. How many bricks can he buy if he has only ₹4000?
questions.explanation: Cost of 1000 bricks = ₹2500. So, cost of 1 brick = ₹2.50. Number of bricks for ₹4000 = 4000 ÷ 2.50 = 1600 bricks.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: If A & B can complete a piece of work in 15 days and B alone in 20 days, in how many days can A alone complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work done by (A + B) in 1 day = 1/15. Work done by B alone in 1 day = 1/20. Work done by A alone in 1 day = 1/15 - 1/20 = (4 - 3)/60 = 1/60. Hence, A alone can complete the work in 60 days.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: What was the mission's name that took Shubhanshu Shukla to the International Space Station?
questions.explanation: ISRO selected Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla for the Axiom Mission 4 (Ax-4) to the International Space Station as part of a collaborative spaceflight agreement.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: From which Upanishad is the phrase 'Satyameva Jayate' taken?
questions.explanation: The national motto of India, 'Satyameva Jayate' (Truth alone triumphs), is a mantra taken from the ancient Mundaka Upanishad.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Which of the following is the longest National Highway in India?
questions.explanation: National Highway 44 (NH-44) is the longest highway in India, traversing from Srinagar in the north to Kanyakumari in the south.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: India shares highest length of border with which neighbouring country?
questions.explanation: India shares its longest international land border with Bangladesh, extending over approximately 4,096 kilometers.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Gir National Park is situated at:
questions.explanation: Gir National Park, renowned as the sole home of the Asiatic lion, is located in the state of Gujarat.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Our National Song 'Vande Mataram' composed by Shri Bankimchandra Chatterji and published in the novel named:
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' was originally a poem written by Bankimchandra Chatterjee in the 1870s, which he later included in his famous 1882 novel 'Anandamath'.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi at the Bombay session of the All-India Congress Committee on August 8, 1942.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Who gave the slogan 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi delivered the famous 'Do or Die' (Karo Ya Maro) speech on August 8, 1942, marking the beginning of the Quit India Movement.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: The autobiography of which of the following famous Indian musicians is titled 'My Life, My Music'?
questions.explanation: Pandit Ravi Shankar, the globally renowned sitar maestro, authored his autobiography titled 'My Music, My Life' (often referenced as 'My Life, My Music' in competitive exams).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: In 71st National Film Awards, which film won the best feature film award?
questions.explanation: In context of the exam paper key, '12th Fail' was chosen as the answer among the options provided.
questions.subject: General Awareness
